title: MCLN EX17 Location Mask Update Mod Spec & Configuration Guide
fullText:
Manhattan Associate s, Inc. Enhancement for McLane MCLN Location Mask Update MOD SLOT - 2019 Contents TOC \o "1-6" \h \z \u 1 Enhancement Revision History PAGEREF _Toc41472058 \h 3 2 Slotting Location Mask Modification PAGEREF _Toc41472059 \h 4 2.1 Summary Information PAGEREF _Toc41472060 \h 4 2.2 Overview PAGEREF _Toc41472061 \h 4 2.3 Slotting Requirements PAGEREF _Toc41472062 \h 4 2.4 Assumptions PAGEREF _Toc41472063 \h 4 2.5 Location Mask Update PAGEREF _Toc41472064 \h 5 2.6 Effected Processes/UI’s PAGEREF _Toc41472065 \h 6 2.7 Platform Information PAGEREF _Toc41472066 \h 6 3 Process Change/Addition PAGEREF _Toc41472067 \h 6 3.1 Enablement PAGEREF _Toc41472068 \h 6 3.2 Validations PAGEREF _Toc41472069 \h 7 3.3 Updates PAGEREF _Toc41472070 \h 7 4 Required Data Setup PAGEREF _Toc41472071 \h 7 4.1 Warehouse Master PAGEREF _Toc41472072 \h 7 4.2 System Codes (C-S01-001) PAGEREF _Toc41472073 \h 7 4.3 Database PAGEREF _Toc41472074 \h 7 4.4 Custom Configurations PAGEREF _Toc41472075 \h 7 4.4.1 System Code Types PAGEREF _Toc41472076 \h 7 4.4.2 System Code Parameters PAGEREF _Toc41472077 \h 8 5 Estimates and Acknowledgement PAGEREF _Toc41472078 \h 8 5.1 Estimated Hours PAGEREF _Toc41472079 \h 8 5.2 Acknowledgement PAGEREF _Toc41472080 \h 8 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: SFDC Stephen Hushak 4 / 1 4 / 20 20 1 .0 Creation 5416509 Stephen Hushak 5/26/2020 2.0 Scope Add: Location Enablement 5416509 Slotting Location Mask Modification Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: Change Request Number: RD-36270 Overview The McLane location mask breakdown is different from the Manhattan Associates breakdown. For McLane their location breakdown is Zone/Aisle/ Bay / Position / Level . The base configuration of this field is Zone/Aisle/Bay/ Level / Position . We need to flip the position and level value on the backend of the tool , anywhere the location is present . This modification spec ifications doc describes how we would solve for this as it pertains to the Slotting Optimization tool. Zone Aisle Bay Position Level Example: 1A-RB-118-53 1A RB 118 5 3 AA A A NNN N N Slotting Requirements Must be able to turn off and turn on the modification using system codes. Must be able to enable the modification on individual pick locations, regardless of location class . All effected Slotting processes will look in the base level field when we are looking for the position value . (specific processes called out below) All effected Slotting processes will look in the base position field when we are looking for the level value . (specific processes called out below) This change will not affect how the bay and level fields are displayed in the UI for any WM or Slotting UI Assumptions Slotting’s base location mask follows the ZAB LP formatting. The custom location mask will follow an Z A B P L formatting. Reserve and dynamic locations are out of scope for this mod. The display location will not be affected by this change. Any location can have more than one location group assignment Th e logic that will change are highlighted below in the Effected UI/Processes Are a When building sequences, we will have to reference the position field for all level sequences When building sequences, we will have to reference the level field for all position sequences When creating f ilters for level, we will create them using the position field When creating filters for position , we will create them using the level field The level and position components will visually stay the same to the users, we will just change the mapping on the backend to read the fields in a reverse fashion. Location Mask Update Slotting O ptimization looks to the level and position fields when assigning items to the appropriate locations. When building rack types, we use the ‘level priority’ field to indicate favorable picking levels (North and South) when picking. With this functionality, we can reduce the amount of bending and reaching a picker does in a given day. Within the reslot logic, this is one of the areas that would be impacted by the code change. 607324 143870 0 Additionally, Slotting uses the position field to determine the sequence of locations (East and West). For example, if we look at the range below, the Slotting Graphical UI automatically sorts our aisles by the bay , and then by position, value ascending from left to right (Bay D107 , Positions 1 – 5 to Bay D126 , Positions 1 - 5 ). Finally, the l evel and position fields are variables that go into the reslot engine for analysis. These are key variables because they are directly tied to our configuration elements. We will alter the logic for all reslot processes to look for the position value in the level field and the level value in the position field. Effected Processes/ UI’s Rack Types: In the R ack T ype UI, the Level Map, Level Priority, and Level fields will all reference the level value. Slotting Graphical UI: In the Slotting Graphical UI when we draw the picture of the aisle, we need to reference both the level and position field s when w e draw the bays and aisles. All R eslot L ogic: For all reslots , we will alter the mapping to look for the position value in the level field and the level value in the position field. P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Slotting Optimization Oracle/Tomcat /RHEL 2 0 1 9 Process Change/Addition Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. The system code determines if the mod is turned on or off. IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement) IF C-S01-00 1 byte 1 = Y ( extension specific enablement) IF WHSE_PARAMETERS.SLOT_IT_USED = ‘ Y ’ THEN the functio nality detailed in this section is enabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled The following section describes how to enable the mod by location using the Location Grouping UI. We will have a slotting specific Group Type / Attribute combination that will need to be configured on the location to flip the location. When this attribute is updated, we will be keying on this field in the LOCN_GRP table. Validations When the extension is disabled or slotting is disabled, the tool should read Zone/Ais le/Bay/Level/Position. When the extension is enabled, the tool should read the location mask Zone/Ais le/ Bay / Position /Level for all locations where location group = Slotting Location Group . Updates The base location mask functionality is not affected, and the only updates will be seen on the backend of the Slotting applicatio n . Required Data Setup Warehouse Master Parameter (Tab > Section > Parameter) Value Misc > Slotting > Slotting I nfo I nstalled? Checked System Codes (C-S01-001) Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 GBLENBL Global Enablement for All Extensions 1 1 Extension On/Off Y/N Flag Y: Enable all mods N: Disable all mods Checked C S01 001 S01 Slotting Location Mask Update 1 1 Extension On/Off Y/N Flag Y: Enable S01 N: Disable S01 Checked Add Code ID SO01 to C-000 D atabase The changes made in the DB will be seen in the SLOT view. Slotting functionality will follow the SLOT view and make the appropriate flip when the mod is enabled. Custom Configurations System Code Types Rec Type Code Type Code Desc RF Desc Max Len. Allow Add Allow Delete Allow Parm Maint Allow Desc Mod WHSE/ BU/ Ent Parm Exist Order by C S01 Modification Enablement MOD 15 Y Y Y Y W Y Code ID Code IDs System Code Parameters Rec Type Code Type From Byte To Byte Parm Desc Parameter Validation C S01 1 1 Extension On/Off Check Box Y/N Flag Estimates and Acknowledgement All services provided are pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Acknowledgement for Consulting Services or the Software Services Agreement previously entered into between our respective companies and these Agreements provide that such services are billed at the hourly billing rates in effect at the time services are rendered. This acknowledgement is to cover the estimate given below. Please note that the estimate below is good for only 60 days from the date the quote is provided. Estimated Hours Solution: Estimated Hours WMS 0 SO 195 Total: 195 Acknowledgement Your signature is required as proof of acceptance of the extension described above and the estimated cost for programming to begin . McLane By: Signature _________________________________ Name: Printed _________________________________ Title: _________________________________ Date: _________________________________
